summary: Travel hotels and activities branches are wired through the existing Amadeus MCP playbook, documented, registered on GKE, and smoke-tested. All five travel intents completed with render widgets; hotels and activities both exercised tool:agent hops into automation/agents/mcp/amadeus. The Amadeus test API continued returning upstream failures for several searches, so the validated widget path is the friendly-error app:column, matching the task's accepted fallback.

ops_primary.title: feat(travel-agent): wire hotels and activities branches via the Amadeus MCP playbook

ops_fix_forward_helper_scope.title: fix(travel-agent): expose classifier coercion helpers to exec globals

ops_fix_forward_activity_coordinates.title: fix(travel-agent): default activity coordinates when classifier omits lat/lon

docs.title: docs(tutorials): note hotels and activities branches in travel agent tutorial

notes: Extended the travel classifier schema across the HTTP classifier, Vertex MCP prompt, and classifier merger with cityCode, latitude, and longitude.
Added amadeus_via_mcp_hotels, render_hotels, amadeus_via_mcp_activities, and render_activities to automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml.
Updated log_classification routing so hotels and activities no longer fall through to render_help.
Pydantic validation passed using the current checkout's model import: noetl.core.dsl.engine.models.executor.Playbook.
Validation exposed the same Python helper visibility issue seen in the earlier Vertex merger; ops PR #62 added globals().update for the helper functions.
Validation then exposed missing activity lat/lon from the classifier on a Times Square query; ops PR #63 added deterministic coordinate fallback for common cities/landmarks and a NYC default.
Tutorial 07 now lists flights, hotels, locations, and activities branches, the shared agent-to-MCP hop pattern, and the new classifier fields.
repos/docs npm install && npm run build completed cleanly before merge.

4_re_register_and_smoke.environment: GKE noetl-cluster, project noetl-demo-19700101, port-forwarded locally on localhost:18082
4_re_register_and_smoke.worker_capacity_note: Initial parallel branch smokes saturated the two-worker pool with parent tool:agent executions, leaving child MCP executions unable to drain promptly. For validation, noetl-worker was temporarily scaled to 6 replicas, fresh smokes were run, and all completed. This is an operational capacity finding for nested agent-heavy flows, not a travel runtime code failure.

smokes.note: Friendly-error widget rendered because the Amadeus test API returned an upstream failure.

smokes.name: hotels
smokes.note: New branch exercised the Amadeus MCP hop and rendered the accepted friendly-error widget.

smokes.name: activities
smokes.note: New branch exercised the Amadeus MCP hop; classifier/fallback supplied Times Square coordinates.

4_re_register_and_smoke.screenshot_note: Browser screenshots were not captured in this smoke-only handoff; canonical widget evidence is the persisted render event path for each execution.

smokes.note: NoETL-level execution completed; upstream Amadeus 500 surfaced as an MCP error envelope.
NoETL-level execution completed; projection exposes flattened data_* fields for the activity response.

lessons: Nested tool:agent fan-out needs spare worker capacity. With two workers, parallel parent agent calls can occupy the pool while child MCP executions wait; validation passed after scaling workers to six and rerunning fresh smokes.
Python helpers referenced by code executed through NoETL's exec-style runner must be exported through globals().update when downstream helper functions need to resolve them.
Classifier schemas that add geo fields need deterministic fallbacks for common natural-language activity locations so downstream MCP calls receive numeric coordinates.
deferred_followups: Extract the duplicated classifier system prompt into a single workload/default field shared by HTTP and Vertex branches.
Investigate Amadeus test API 500s separately from travel runtime wiring.
Capacity/autoscaling follow-up for nested tool:agent heavy playbooks so parent and child executions cannot starve each other.
Item #3: app:form widget for refining Amadeus filters before rerunning.
